channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trent University Study Highlights the Challenges of AI Use in Classrooms
item.description: A recent study from Trent University reveals that the widespread use of artificial intelligence among Canadian post-secondary students is creating significant challenges for educators. Professors repo...

item.title: Train Collides with School Bus in Gilgil, Pupils Injured
item.description: In Gilgil, Nakuru County, a school trip turned into a shocking incident on Friday, May 15, when a train collided with a school bus belonging to Teresia’s School at a railway crossing along the Gilgil–...

item.title: DOJ sets up committee to investigate Senate shooting incident
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has officially formed a special panel to investigate the recent gunfire incident that occurred at the Senate. The move comes as authorities aim to thoroughly examine th...

item.title: Hisbah arrests six people, including married woman, during hotel raid in Kebbi
item.description: For Kebbi State, officers of the Hisbah Board, wey dey operate under Ministry of Religious Affairs, don arrest six people after dem carry out one raid for hotel wey dey behind Presidential Lodge for B...

item.title: Education Minister explains NEET-UG 2026 issues and plans computer-based exams from next year
item.description: The NEET-UG 2026 examination has faced significant controversy due to technical issues experienced during the OMR-based test. According to the Education Minister, the main reason behind the disruption...

item.title: Xi-Trump summit highlights potential for US-China cooperation amid Taiwan tensions
item.description: The recent summit in Beijing between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping sought to address trade, technology, and regional security concerns, but the issue of Taiwan remained th...

item.title: Trump Considers Mass Pardons Amidst Birthday and Political Distractions
item.description: The article discusses speculation around U.S. President Donald Trump potentially issuing 250 pardons on June 14, 2026, coinciding with both Flag Day and his 80th birthday. The author argues that the t...

item.title: Defense Secretary Cancels Planned U.S. Troop Deployment to Poland, Raising European Security Concerns
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth unexpectedly canceled a planned deployment of 4,000 U.S. troops to Poland, catching both Pentagon officials and European allies by surprise. Troops and equipment from Te...

item.title: Moody’s Confirms Tanzania’s Stable B1 Rating as Key Sectors Drive Growth
item.description: Moody’s Ratings has confirmed Tanzania’s long-term issuer rating at B1 with a stable outlook, showing confidence in the country’s economy despite political turbulence in 2025. The economy is projected...

item.title: The 1937 Bugatti Type 57 Cabriolet: A Masterpiece of Automotive Design
item.description: The 1937 Bugatti Type 57 Cabriolet remains one of the most iconic examples of pre-war automotive design, merging high-performance engineering with artistic elegance. Primarily designed by Jean Bugatti...

item.title: DOJ confirms Dela Rosa faces arrest if he leaves the Philippines amid ICC warrant
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has confirmed that former Philippine police chief Ronald dela Rosa will be subject to arrest should he attempt to leave the country. This comes after the International ...

item.title: Behind the Scenes: Julia Stiles and the Making of '10 Things I Hate About You'
item.description: The 1999 teen film '10 Things I Hate About You' has become a defining piece of late-1990s pop culture, largely thanks to Julia Stiles’ portrayal of the independent and intellectual Kat Stratford. Film...

item.title: DepEd distributes mid-year bonuses to public school personnel ahead of next school year
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has started releasing mid-year bonuses for more than one million public school personnel, including around 900,000 teachers and over 100,000 non-teaching staff nati...

item.title: Adani to Pay $18 Million Settlement in US Civil Case Without Admitting Guilt
item.description: Indian billionaire Gautam Adani has agreed to a settlement worth $18 million in a US civil court case related to corruption allegations, without admitting any wrongdoing. The 2024 New York indictment ...

item.title: Pakistan Requests Early Launch of Direct Flights with US Amid Strengthened Ties
item.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi stated on Friday that the Pakistani government is actively pursuing the early launch of direct flights between Pakistan and the United States. During a meeting with US A...

item.title: US-China Cooperation Raises Global Concerns Over Hidden Costs
item.description: The recent summit between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping marks a new phase in the relationship between the world's two largest economies. While the term 'G2' once symbolise...

item.title: Vincent Co reportedly in the Philippines amid wedding rumors with Bea Alonzo in Spain
item.description: Reports surfaced on May 15, 2026, that Vincent Co was seen in the Philippines on the same day that rumors suggested he was getting married to actress Bea Alonzo in Spain. Despite circulating speculati...

item.title: Iran sets conditions for renewed US talks and asserts control over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tensions between Iran and the United States remain high as Tehran has outlined five conditions for returning to negotiations, including reparations for the ongoing conflict and formal recognition of I...

item.title: Ombudsman Suspends Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca for 6 Months After Firing Incident
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman, led by Jesus Crispin Remulla, has placed acting Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca under a preventive suspension of six months following a shooting incident inside the Se...

item.title: Court Approves Withdrawal of Corruption Charges Against Former Migori Governor Okoth Obado After Asset Forfeiture Deal
item.description: Former Migori Governor Zachary Okoth Obado has achieved a significant legal success after a Nairobi anti-corruption court approved the withdrawal of all corruption charges against him and 17 other co-...
